mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings expectations and lowers equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending economic contraction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and housing starts indicates a supply-demand mismatch and inventory accumulation, which compresses corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST shows no statistical significance in predicting 20-day returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a baseline INDPRO-only model in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with elevated federal funds rates compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite simultaneous CPI and FEDFUNDS increases, or the combined signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects strong consumer spending, which directly boosts near-term corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive RSAFS surprises are not statistically different from zero, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% in a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 3.0%, borrowing costs suppress business investment and consumer credit, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the signal fails to generate a positive information coefficient in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market recovery, which supports consumer spending and equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal does not outperform a univariate PAYEMS model in out-of-sample prediction, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 110 signals manufacturing sector contraction and supply chain disruptions, which reduce corporate earnings and cause negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 60% of cases following an INDPRO drop below 110, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
